prospect.name: Desange Miami
prospect.notes: Editorial Italian coiffeur; Design District flagship.

evidence.claim: Editorial black-and-white photography, restrained palette, serif typography.
evidence.excerpt: Interiors rendered in travertine and brushed brass; zero discount messaging anywhere on site.

evidence.claim: Instagram grid reads like a magazine spread — couture references, in-salon shoots with stylists in tailored uniforms.
evidence.excerpt: Editorial-grade image style consistent across 60+ recent posts.

evidence.claim: Salon interior photography reveals private suites with curated art, layered lighting, and upholstered seating.
Menu organized as rituals (not à-la-carte), each framed with a narrative description.
evidence.excerpt: “The Desange Signature Ritual — a 90-minute immersion beginning with a bespoke scalp consultation.”

evidence.claim: Offers a dedicated scalp & hair wellness menu distinct from cutting/color.
Carries Oribe, Christophe Robin, and a small curated Italian line. Retail shelf visible above shampoo area.
Dedicated 'Home Ritual' page framing retail as care continuity rather than upsell.
Explicit scalp-wellness framing: 'treat the scalp as you would the skin.'
evidence.excerpt: Scalp treatments positioned alongside cutting/color as core offerings, not add-ons.

evidence.claim: Founder Q&A references a multi-year practice rooted in Italian hair care philosophy.
Design District Miami address — adjacent to Hermès, Dior, Bulgari flagships.
Pricing from $160 for cut, $420+ for signature color.
Reviews reference repeat visits during Art Basel and Miami Swim Week; named stylists receive specific praise.
evidence.excerpt: “Worth every flight from Paris — Luca is a true artist.”

evidence.claim: Flagship footprint appears ~3,200 sq ft with private rooms visible in interior photography.
Already offers wash-house services in a dedicated rear area with plumbing.

categories.missing_data_note: Interior dimensions not explicitly published; a site visit would confirm room availability.

evidence.claim: Single flagship, but team of 14 stylists and a named director.
350+ Google reviews at 4.9 avg; steady press coverage in Vogue and Ocean Drive.
Team size × average ticket × booking density suggests revenue comfortably above the $1M threshold.
Brand feels mutually elevating — no mass-market cues, no groupon history, visible editorial discipline.

evidence_payload.summary_note: Strong alignment across aesthetic, wellness, and affluence signals. Operational confirmation via site visit advised.
